title: pyrus × magdeburgensis
intro: Pyrus × magdeburgensis, also known as the Magdeburg pear, is a sterile hybrid plant resulting from the cross between Pyrus communis and Pyrus serotina. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Pyrus × magdeburgensis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive problems, and provide relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be taken internally to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
